STACK.UI_LIBRARY: shadcn/ui (Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, Lucide Icons)
STACK.LLM_PROVIDER: Groq API (Chat Completions: llama-3.1-70b-versatile or llama3-8b-8192 for fast response, direct calls with system prompt/negative-prompt guarding)

ARCHITECTURE.OVERVIEW: A client-server application where the frontend is built in Next.js and deployed to Vercel, with Next.js serverless API routes handling the logic. Data persistence is managed via Supabase. Direct, structured LLM prompt engineering with the Groq API powers mood analysis and the conversational companion. No RAG is utilized. To safeguard user interactions, a dedicated safety middleware/pre-check intercepts messages on the backend before they reach the LLM.

COMPONENTS.COMPONENT: Provides an intuitive form interface using shadcn/ui components (slider or emoji buttons) and a text area for writing. Submits to `/api/logs` where a Groq API prompt parses the journal text to extract emotional themes and triggers.

Queries `/api/logs` to retrieve the student's log history. Displays a 7-day mood trend chart (using Recharts) and a frequency-based tag cloud of extracted study triggers (e.g. "Physics Test", "Mock Exam", "Peer Pressure").

An interactive, chat-bubble interface. Bounded by strict system guidelines to focus strictly on student exam-prep anxiety and wellness. Non-wellness queries are rejected gracefully using negative prompting.

A local utility in Next.js serverless functions checking incoming user chat messages for self-harm or emergency triggers. If triggered, disables standard LLM call, saves a crisis log, and displays a prominent red warning card with Indian mental wellness helplines (Kiran, AASRA).

TABLE.name: daily_logs
TABLE.DESCRIPTION: Stores daily mood and journal entries along with LLM-extracted sentiment analytics.

COLUMN.name: user_id
COLUMN.description: Identifies the student session or Supabase auth user

COLUMN.name: mood
COLUMN.description: Emoji indicator or text representation of user mood

COLUMN.name: dominant_emotion
COLUMN.description: Extracted dominant emotion (e.g. Anxiety, Hope, Stress)

COLUMN.name: triggers
COLUMN.description: Extracted exam preparation stress triggers (e.g. Physics, time limits)

TABLE.name: chat_history
TABLE.DESCRIPTION: Stores interactive chat messages between the student and the wellness companion.

COLUMN.name: message
COLUMN.description: The message submitted by the student

COLUMN.name: response
COLUMN.description: The empathetic response generated by Groq (or safety bypass statement)

REPOSITORY_STRUCTURE.DIRECTORY_TREE: ├── public/ │ ├── assets/ # Icons, mascots, illustrations │ └── favicon.ico ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Core page layout, themes, global providers │ │ ├── page.tsx # Welcome page/landing (session initialization) │ │ ├── dashboard/ │ │ │ └── page.tsx # Student dashboard showing trend analysis and trigger clouds │ │ ├── log/ │ │ │ └── page.tsx # Low-friction mood logging and journal writing form │ │ ├── chat/ │ │ │ └── page.tsx # Empathetic conversational chat view │ │ └── api/ # Next.js Serverless API Routes │ │ ├── logs/ │ │ │ └── route.ts # GET logs/analytics, POST logs (with Groq extraction) │ │ └── chat/ │ │ └── route.ts # POST chat messages (safety bypass and guarded Groq response) │ ├── components/ # React Components │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui generic building blocks │ │ │ ├── button.tsx │ │ │ ├── card.tsx │ │ │ ├── dialog.tsx │ │ │ ├── slider.tsx │ │ │ ├── textarea.tsx │ │ │ └── alert.tsx │ │ ├── navbar.tsx # Standard header & menu navigation │ │ ├── mood-chart.tsx # Recharts LineChart for 7-day mood representation │ │ ├── trigger-cloud.tsx# Tag cloud component rendering stress counts │ │ ├── chat-window.tsx # Scrollable message area with scroll-lock helpers │ │ ├── chat-bubble.tsx # Message message bubble (Student vs Assistant) │ │ └── crisis-card.tsx # Warning banner / helpline details card (Kiran, AASRA) │ ├── lib/ # Core server/client utilities │ │ ├── supabase.ts # Supabase client instantiation │ │ ├── groq.ts # Groq API client config & prompt helper functions │ │ └── safety.ts # Crisis keyword patterns & bypass evaluation check │ └── types/ # TypeScript declarations │ └── index.ts # Shared typescript interfaces (Log, Message, Stats) ├── components.json # shadcn configuration file ├── next.config.js # Next.js setup configuration ├── package.json # Root dependency configuration ├── tailwind.config.js # CSS styling configurations └── tsconfig.json # Typescript configurations

MILESTONE.name: Database Setup & Supabase Integration
MILESTONE.DESCRIPTION: Create and configure Supabase project, execute SQL tables DDL, set up indexes and RLS policies, and implement Supabase Client initialization in src/lib/supabase.ts.

DELIVERABLES.DELIVERABLE: Execute SQL DDL statements for `daily_logs` and `chat_history` in Supabase SQL editor.
Implement Supabase connection pool and TS client in `src/lib/supabase.ts` reading env credentials.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA_MAP.AC: Requires DB tables to be ready for saving mood entries.

MILESTONE.name: Serverless API Endpoints & Groq Integration
MILESTONE.DESCRIPTION: Develop Next.js API routes `/api/logs` and `/api/chat`. Integrate Groq API for prompt-based trigger extraction and guarded wellness conversations. Implement safety checking logic.

DELIVERABLES.DELIVERABLE: Develop `/api/logs` POST (extracts dominant emotion/triggers via Groq and inserts log) and GET (fetches history & counts).
Develop `/api/chat` POST (intercepts crisis keywords, or calls Groq with strict prompts and records chat history).
Implement safety verification parser in `src/lib/safety.ts` with test coverage for crisis keywords.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA_MAP.AC: Backend saves entry, calls Groq parser, and stores triggers.
Bypass verification intercepts and triggers emergency warning resources on backend.
Groq companion prompt enforces WHO/MoHFW wellness alignment and grounding citation.

MILESTONE.name: Frontend Journaling & Analytics Dashboard
MILESTONE.DESCRIPTION: Create Next.js pages for mood logging and student dashboard. Integrate Recharts to render the 7-day mood tracker and compile the trigger word cloud.

DELIVERABLES.DELIVERABLE: Create `/log` page with slider/emoji mood selection and journal text entry form linked to `/api/logs` POST.
Create `/dashboard` page with LineChart for daily trends and styled tag buttons for prep triggers.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA_MAP.AC: User logs mood and text, and sees the trigger tags populate their Dashboard analytics.

MILESTONE.name: Conversational Companion Interface & Crisis UI
MILESTONE.DESCRIPTION: Develop Chat UI panel with smooth message threads, load indicators, and the crisis warning overlay.

DELIVERABLES.DELIVERABLE: Create `/chat` interface with a persistent scrolling pane of previous/current session messages.
Build conditional UI to render the red Crisis Card containing Kiran/AASRA helpline numbers when the API flags a crisis.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA_MAP.AC: Student entering self-harm phrases is immediately blocked with the Crisis Card and Indian helpline list.
Student queries result in exam-stress advice grounded in WHO/MoHFW protocols with citations visible in the bubble.

MILESTONE.name: End-to-End Validation, Hardening & Launch
MILESTONE.DESCRIPTION: Perform strict integration testing across edge scenarios, configure production variables, and host the platform live on Vercel.

DELIVERABLES.DELIVERABLE: Execute verify scripts for API pathways (logs, chat, bypass, metrics).
Publish production instance on Vercel connected to live Supabase DB and Groq production token.
